findings.description: Missing Content-Security-Policy header
findings.risk: The browser has no restrictions on which scripts can run on the page. If the site is compromised, injected scripts can operate without constraint.

findings.description: WordPress detected (version not determined)
findings.risk: The site runs WordPress. While the exact version is not exposed, CMS-specific attack patterns can still be attempted.

findings.description: 4 WordPress plugins detected
findings.risk: Each plugin is additional code from a third-party developer. Outdated or abandoned plugins are a common entry point for attackers. Plugins should be reviewed and kept updated.

findings.description: Backend technology exposed: MySQL, PHP
findings.risk: The server advertises which backend technologies it runs. This gives attackers information about which exploits may be applicable. It does not mean the site is vulnerable, but it reduces the effort needed to find an attack vector.

findings.description: WordPress theme detected: Hello Elementor
findings.risk: The active theme is visible in the HTML source. Outdated themes are a common source of vulnerabilities, similar to plugins.
